highlights_headline: SkiaSharp 4.151.0 delivers allocation-free hot paths in HarfBuzzSharp and SKColor, plus upstream Skia m151 bug fixes.
highlights_body: This release focuses on managed-layer performance: zero-allocation UTF-8 encoding in HarfBuzzSharp.Buffer, pooled char arrays in tag parsing, zero-copy shaper glyph spans, and a ReadOnlySpan<char> SKColor hex parser. It also merges two rounds of upstream Skia m151 fixes.

bullets.detail: Two rounds of upstream chrome/m151 fixes were merged, bringing stability and rendering improvements from the Skia team.

bullets.lead: SKColor hex parsing gains ReadOnlySpan<char> overloads
bullets.detail: SKColor.Parse and TryParse now accept ReadOnlySpan<char>, avoiding string allocations when parsing hex color strings from sliced input.
The unnecessary unsafe modifier has been removed from GRContextOptions, reducing noise for consumers building with -warnaserror.
The per-call UTF-8 encoding previously allocated a temporary byte array on every call. It now uses a pooled ArrayPool<byte> buffer, eliminating allocations on hot text-shaping paths.

bullets.lead: Tag parsing no longer allocates a char[4] per call
bullets.detail: HarfBuzzSharp.Tag.Parse and SKFourByteTag.Parse previously allocated a small char array on every parse. Both now use stack-allocated spans.

bullets.lead: SKShaper glyph results read via zero-copy spans
bullets.detail: The SKShaper result retrieval path previously copied glyph data into managed arrays. It now uses Span-based reads directly over the native buffer.

contributor_summaries.ramezgerges: Cross-backend golden-image visual regression test harness
